title: Sphaerostephanos taiwanensis
taxonomy: Sphaerostephanos taiwanensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Sphaerostephanos, which is a group of ferns that are known for their rounded or spherical sori. The Dryopteridaceae family is a large and diverse group of ferns that are found in many parts of the world. Sphaerostephanos taiwanensis is a specific species that is native to Taiwan, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The classification of Sphaerostephanos taiwanensis is based on a combination of morphological and molecular evidence.

intro: Sphaerostephanos taiwanensis, also known as the Taiwanese spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiproliferative properties, which may aid in the prevention and treatment of various diseases.
